title: "I felt guilty for being the chosen one": Ada Ferrer on her memoir about leaving Cuba
description: When she fled Cuba, Ada Ferrer's mother took only one of her two children. In her new memoir, Keeper of My Kin, Ferrer grapples with that decision's reverberations across generations of her family.
hook: Ada Ferrer's mother could only take one child fleeing Cuba. Decades later, she writes the family reckoning that absence demanded.

title: The Expansive Joy of Mao Ishikawa
description: The photographer doesn’t sentimentalize her subjects; she pays attention to them.
hook: Mao Ishikawa photographs people without myth-making—just sustained, unsentimental attention that feels like an act of love.

title: How Douglas Latchford Trafficked Cambodia’s Gods Into Western Museums
hook: How a Western collector turned sacred Cambodian statues into inventory, and what that theft reveals about desire masquerading as taste.

title: When the Religious Right Came for Martin Scorsese
description: “The Last Temptation of Christ,” the director’s 1988 epic, became one of the first victims of a new conservative playbook—and set the template for the culture wars of today.
hook: Scorsese's 1988 Christ film didn't start the culture wars—but the right's playbook against it predicted everything that followed.

title: Lacan in Tirana
description:
‘Psychoanalysis never truly took root in Albania during the early decades of the twentieth century and, after the communist takeover in 1944, it was actively suppressed by the regime.’

Ardian Vehbiu on Jacques Lacan’s visit to Albania.

The post Lacan in Tirana appeared first on Granta.
hook: When Lacan landed in Cold War Albania, psychoanalysis met a regime determined to suppress the very idea of an interior life.

title: 'American Rambler' traces the path, and truth-squads the tale, of Johnny Appleseed
description: Isaac Fitzgerald, author of Dirtbag, Massachusetts, grew up with tales of local townie Johnny Appleseed. So when he found himself in need of a long, mind-clearing walk, he traced the legend's path.
hook: Isaac Fitzgerald walked Johnny Appleseed's real path and found the legend bears almost no resemblance to the man who lived it.

title: Chemical Romance: The Light-Sensitive Materials of Sapphire Goss
description:
Sapphire Goss talks with Bernie Brooks and Kristen Gallerneaux about the materials, magic, and catharsis at the heart of her debut album, Light Sensitive Materials

Screenshot

“I’m using optical sound effects. For people that don’t know, optical sound used to be to the side of the image on the film strip. When I was working with archives, th
hook: Sapphire Goss uses light-sensitive film like a séance—materials that transform themselves, unpredictable and beautiful and alive.
